Therefore, recommend using two-stage multinomial bootstrap percentile confidence intervals (Pustejovsky et al., 2025). Furthermore, obtaining accurate confidence intervals requires using relatively large number bootstrap replications (Davidson & MacKinnon, 2000); using insufficient number produce confidence intervals narrow -nominal coverage. recommend using 1999 replications, default used selection_model() function bootstrap argument set "two-stage", "multinomial", "exponential". computational demands bootstrapping can mitigated using parallel processing, demonstrate .

title: Meta-analytic selection models with cluster-robust and cluster-bootstrap standard errors for dependent effect size estimates
text: Selective reporting occurs statistically significant, affirmative results likely reported (therefore likely available meta-analysis) compared null, non-affirmative results. Selective reporting major concern research syntheses distorts evidence base available meta-analysis, skewing meta-analytic averages toward favorable findings misrepresenting true population effects. Failure account selective reporting can lead inflated effect size estimates meta-analysis biased estimates heterogeneity, making difficult draw accurate conclusions synthesis. many tools available already investigate correct selective reporting. Widely used methods include: graphical diagnostics like funnel plots, tests adjustments funnel plot asymmetry like trim--fill, Egger’s regression, PET/PEESE, selection models, pp-value diagnostics. However, methods investigating selective reporting can accommodate dependent effect sizes. limitation poses problem meta-analyses education, psychology social sciences, dependent effects common feature meta-analytic data. Dependent effect sizes occur primary studies report results multiple measures outcome construct, collect repeated measures outcome across multiple time-points, involve comparisons multiple intervention conditions. Ignoring dependency effect size estimates included meta-analysis leads overly narrow confidence intervals, hypothesis tests inflated type one error rates, incorrect inferences. Pustejovsky, Citkowicz, Joshi (2025) developed examined methods investigating accounting selective reporting meta-analytic models also account dependent effect sizes. simulation results show combining selection models robust variance estimation account dependent effects reduces bias estimate overall effect size. Combining selection models cluster bootstrapping leads confidence intervals close--nominal coverage rates. metaselection package provides implementation several meta-analytic selection models. main function, selection_model(), fits step function beta density selection models. handle dependence effect size estimates, function provides options use cluster-robust (sandwich) variance estimation cluster bootstrapping assess uncertainty model parameter estimates.

title: Meta-analytic selection models with cluster-robust and cluster-bootstrap standard errors for dependent effect size estimates
text: Several existing meta-analysis packages provide implementations selection models, limited assumption independent effects. metafor package (Viechtbauer 2010) includes selmodel() function, allows users fit many different types selection models. weightr package (Coburn Vevea 2019) includes functions estimate class pp-value selection models described Vevea Hedges (1995). However, packages assume effect sizes independent, results produce incorrect standard errors misleadingly narrow confidence intervals datasets containing multiple effects drawn study. addition, PublicationBias package (Braginsky, Mathur, VanderWeele 2023) implements sensitivity analyses selective reporting bias incorporate cluster-robust variance estimation methods handling dependent effect sizes. However, sensitivity analyses implemented package based pre-specified degree selective reporting, rather allowing degree selection estimated data. sensitivity analyses also based specific simple form selection model, allow consideration complex forms selection functions. metaselection package goes beyond tools considering complex forms selective reporting correcting selective reporting bias accommodating meta-analytic datasets include dependent effect sizes.
